name: Clerk
security: Never expose CLERK_SECRET_KEY to the client — it's a server-only secret.
Verify webhook signatures via the Svix-compatible verifier shipped with @clerk/nextjs.
Always call auth() on the server before reading or writing user-owned data.
Use the Clerk middleware matcher to protect routes — do not rely on client-side guards alone.
Sync Clerk user IDs as the canonical ID into your own DB; never use email as the join key.

patterns.name: clerkMiddleware()
patterns.description: Protect routes at the edge with clerkMiddleware and explicit matcher patterns.

patterns.name: Server-side user reads
patterns.description: Use auth() and currentUser() in Server Components and Route Handlers.

patterns.name: Webhook user sync
patterns.description: Listen to user.created / user.updated / user.deleted and mirror into your database.

patterns.name: Organizations / B2B
patterns.description: Enable multi-tenant orgs with role-based access via has({ permission }).
